title: mystacinus ovatus
intro: Mystacinus ovatus, also known as the sweet root or New Zealand gentian, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mystacinus ovatus include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Mystacinus ovatus is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
